name: HammaDev
tagline: Project memory that follows your AI coding agents
description: HammaDev is free, local project memory for Codex, Claude Code, and Grok. Run `hamma`, choose an agent, and continue with decisions, verification, Git state, and one clear next action intact. Native lifecycle hooks checkpoint before compaction and recover exact sessions without a HammaDev account, cloud backend, telemetry, or transcript upload. Built with GPT-5.6 inside Codex during OpenAI Build Week; model-agnostic at runtime.
firstComment:
Hey Product Hunt 👋

I built HammaDev because every coding-agent switch forced me to reconstruct the same project state: what changed, which decisions still mattered, whether tests actually passed, and what the next agent should do.

HammaDev gives that memory to the repository instead of one vendor session. Run `hamma`, choose Codex, Claude Code, or Grok, and continue with a compact local brief containing the goal, verified work, Git state, risks, and one clear next action.

During OpenAI Build Week I used Codex as the hands-on engineering agent and GPT-5.6 for the harder reasoning: challenging the evidence/readiness rules, replaying false continuations, and finding cases where completed work could restart. HammaDev existed before the event as a working handoff prototype; the gallery and engineering log identify exactly what shipped during Build Week.

What is in the beta today:
• one-command guided setup
• native lifecycle checkpoints and exact-session recovery
• local repository memory with bounded context
• evidence provenance and Git drift detection
• no HammaDev account, backend, telemetry, or transcript upload

It is free and open source. I would especially value feedback on two questions: would you trust reviewable lifecycle hooks for this workflow, and what evidence would you need before letting a new agent continue automatically?

Thanks for taking a look — I’ll be here all day answering technical questions and testing edge cases with you.
